description: If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for Activate?

Feature Caveat: Activate - If there is no user configuration for DNS servers and HPE Aruba Networking Central is configured, the switch defaults to using Google's DNS server at 8.8.8.8. HPE Aruba Networking Central is enabled by default across all switch platforms.

description: When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers in HPE Aruba AOS-CX 10.15.1050?
For secure classifier policy modifications, HPE Aruba Networking strongly encourages a three-step process: bring down the port, make the modifications, and then bring the port back up.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for CMF?
CMF - No other checkpoint besides 'startup-configuration' gets migrated during the upgrade process.

description: When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When downloading a hot-patch file using the switch WebUI, log messages may incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Do not use reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services for different services. When two services use the same port, unexpected behaviors may occur. Best practice is to assign a unique port for each service across the system.

description: Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what RADIUS caveat is documented?
Feature Caveat: RADIUS - Authorization by means of HPE VSAs is not supported.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what caveat is documented for REST?
REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what is the caveat related to SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.
symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured.
scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log.
workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

description: Symptom: The switch may reboot with the following failure messages: Software thermal reset Insufficient fans Fan tray -1/4/1 faulted with the reason "common error". Scenario: This issue occurs due to resource contention or high software stress. Workaround: Update to a different code version.

section: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
task: Manual Configuration Restore for Software Downgrade
procedure: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy).
Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config.
Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.
description: Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, how do you restore configuration after a software downgrade?

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Use the show checkpoint command to see the saved checkpoints and ensure that you have a checkpoint that is an exact match of the target software version (see the Image Version column in the output of the command, for example, PL.10.xx.yyyy). 2. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

Downgrade Restore: To restore configuration after a software downgrade, follow the documented procedure in order: 1. Copy the backup checkpoint into the startup-config. 3. Boot the switch to the target version (lower version), making sure to select no when prompted to save the current configuration.

procedure: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.

Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.

When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
description: Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any devices updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, how do you perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.

messages.content: For 6100 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what are the steps to perform the software upgrade?

Upgrade Procedure: To perform the software upgrade, follow these steps in order:
1. Copy the new image into the non-current boot bank on the switch using your preferred method.
2. Depending on the version being updated, there may be device component updates needed. Preview any device updates needed using the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command and entering n when asked to continue.
3. When ready to update the system, if a non-failsafe device update is needed, make sure the system will not have any power interruption during the process. Invoke the allow non-failsafe updates command to allow updates to proceed after a switch reboot. Proceed to step 4 within the configured time.
4. Use the boot system <BOOT-BANK> command to initiate the upgrade. On the switch console port, output similar to the documented example will be displayed as various components are being updated.
